item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With all the power the foundation has how does the blog (and the domain) even still exist? Unless there is something anomalous about the blog itself, it seems to me the foundation should have wiped it off the internet.

It would make more sense if, instead of being tied to a domain, he jumps from forum to forum or setting up new blogs all the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: anyone else up for a road trip to Bitch Mountain?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A disturbing and well writted article, I think that this work reflex some of the wicked aspects from the people behavior in Internet.

They aren't just trolls…

Good work sir +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who is ExplodingPopTart and why is he resistant to the effects?
Or are there no effects, and those guys are just evil?
I don't think they're just evil, they did D-Class testing and that D wasn't evil before…
But once in a while, you get these people who can anomalously resist anomalies. Why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it! Really gives a 4chan-esque feeling when reading the comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is "Anonymus" a typo?

item.title: Re: Unfriended II: An SCP Tale.
item.content:encoded: http://www.cinemablend.com/new/Unfriended-2-Happening-Who-Knows-What-Social-Media-Horrors-Await-71118.html

item.title: Unfriended II: An SCP Tale.
item.content:encoded: Make it happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the SCP wiki, not some generic creepypasta site. Some of the swearing seems a bit excessive, especially when I may or may not be viewing this on a school computer. (but I also have another tab open about depression-but that's for schoolwork)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a brilliant idea, but I think it would be better off as a creepypasta rather than an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP conceptually, and I very much like the tone throughout for about 3/4s of the aticle, but I think the ending sequence is far too on-the-nose and directly explanatory. I love the idea of some kind of self-abasing entity somehow invoked by internet hate and I think a clumsy puppetmaster/expositionary tract actually kind of takes away from that. Evening out at a novote for me because I really do like this concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the scip is a guy that mutilates himself for his blog and gets mutilated that heals at the end of every video, and can only be found by fans because his location changes when non fans get too close?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The third "Anonymous" is misspelt in the first video excerpt. Unless it's a user with the name Anonymus. Good read otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
John has the same fetish as me.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you mean "necromancy", maybe? This article wasn't a coldpost (the author post cites a few people for feedback).

item.title: Review.
item.content:encoded:
This was amazing! I love the commentary on this SCP because it reminds me of all those times the internet has made fun of people causing the people to dig them self into a deeper hole. I think this is great and plus I love things that make you crazy in a creative way!

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is a coldpost but there are TONS of people on the internet that make tasteless references to Nazis. It's kinda realistic in my eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is, I feel, quite disturbing. A horrifying display of the horrors of new media.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THAT SOAB CAN DIE IN A FIRE!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one, I just feel like there's something I'm missing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TL;DR

then john was a zombie

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good but… It's disgusting XD
I found it very interesting, i liked it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've made me curious. Which comment is that? The circular saw one? I bet it's the circular saw one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well then… Interesting~!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for not going AWCY? with this. Not every asshole anomalous artist has to be AWCY?

As for the slurs and such, well, you've captured the cesspit of online comment sections nicely. Really, it's almost too clean.

Random experiment! Looks like my top youtube recommendation is a video about Ed Gein (yes, the serial killer) — the top comment starts with "If Satan got a color he white lmao" and quickly devolves to … no, never mind, I feel dirty just reading that. Let's just say that the words "tar baby jungle bunnies" were used, and that's the least offensive part — this is on a video about a serial killer. Like, a proper Foundation level serious documentary.

If that's what a scientific video drags out, this scip is far cleaner than the internet really is. (Note, please don't make it worse though, I really do feel like youtube made me need a shower.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for the casual Nazi reference, it adds nothing to it and is kind of tasteless imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this could do with a bit less information given. It was wonderfully mysterious and unexplained, and fairly spooky at that, but it lost something in the video log where it's shown that there's someone behind 'John'. Upvoted nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't really like it. Seems like a generic mind-affecting SCP to me. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Deep-style video celeb fans and haters… of a guy who can't die. Prooty cool. Yeah.
Somehow almost too vernacular, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's still a no-vote for me (for now, at least), but I really like this interpretation.

item.title: Re: hmmmm
item.content:encoded: I disagree with this quite strongly, and I'm one of the people the slur 'faggot' applies to. It just totally breaks my suspension of disbelief for the Foundation to censor something like this, even if they found it incredibly offensive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty disturbing… Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is interesting. I do feel like the dates, locations, and usernames feel oddly specific for an SCP. Maybe censoring some of those things could give it a bit of fidelity and anonymity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of all the SCPs I have read, this is my favorite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Hmm. Just sounds like a description of /b/. Nothing really special."
Until I see:

D-90327 hands over the paper. Upon closer inspection, it is a poem titled "Fuck John".

Upvoted.
For a first attempt at a humanoid skip, I think its pretty good, amazing actually. Keep up the good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Started kinda "meh", got more interesting, plummeted into "blegh" with the final transcript. It's like Jigsaw is giving heavy-handed moralization about something where not even HE'S sure what the point is.

Also, if EVERYONE who regularly views the blog becomes a -2, and EVERY -2 posts content about the blog whenever possible, that's a huge infohazard spread, which doesn't seem to be addressed at all. It really ought to be a certain % of people who regularly read it become -2s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanted to not like this because it felt like an internet morality play about the dangers of 4chan, but… it's actually pretty disturbing, and in the good horror story way, not the eeeeeeee language and ideas outside of my control make me uncomfortable way.

I particularly like Pig's interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of net-based anomalies, because I often find online communities quite unsettling, but I think this falls short of the mark in a good few places.

Firstly, I think that the central idea of a human who anomalously makes people want to hurt him isn't particularly original.

Secondly, as some others have said, the Foundation response to this is really weird. There's not much to indicate the actual anomalous status of -1 and -2, nothing at all for 2089 itself, and the only really anomalous part, the geographical anomaly, is not categorised at all. And if -1 and -2 are anomalous, the Foundation should be at least attempting to contain them or formulate some kind of response.

Thirdly, I feel like this is really poorly structured, going between transcripts of online activity, officious description, interview, and field exploration without any kind of real coherence.

Fourthly, all the quotations of online discussion feel off to me. I'm not sure if this is because they don't read quite like accurate online interaction, or because online interaction style will always look strange when juxtaposed with more structured and formal writing; in any case, it feels awkward and laboured.

And finally, I find this actually approaching disturbing. Not in the good way, but in the way where I worry slightly about the author. I feel like there's a hell of a lot of descriptions of violence and torture which is unnecessary and bordering on gratuitous. And the casual use of the word 'faggot' made me feel rather uncomfortable.

So… yeah. This didn't chime with me at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The way the Foundation works, shouldn't the 'area that shifts in front of your own eyes' be also part of the SCP, and contained somehow?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote. I really like the concept, but it feels jumbled and cluttered and I lost interest before the end.

Knives, needles and other sharp objects coated in blood. DNA analysis proves that it is a Caucasian male, aged 18.

It would be possible in principle to predict age from telomeres, but because of post-mortem degradation of DNA it's really not accurate. I guess it's fine for the Foundation, but I would suggest a rough age estimate instead of an exact 18.

Also grammatically I would say ''…that this blood is from a Caucasian…''

item.title: hmmmm
item.content:encoded: yknow , i get why you put it there and i understand what it adds to the skip but could you consider [EXPLETIVE REDACT]ing or blackboxing the "faggots"? its a bit too harsh, almost and even if it was redacted, its still obvious enough to know what theyre calling john

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this, do you mind if I ask which one of the comments is the real one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Telomeres.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was gonna no-vote on this one, but then I got to the implication that

Spoiler
Aryanne is the one putting John through this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't Aryanne that Nazi Pony OC?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"ExplodingPopTart: What the hell is wrong with you sick, demented people? You're telling me you ENJOY seeing an innocent person get brutally murdered?

Aryanne:

Get a load of this guy."

Upvoted for accurate and realistic representation of the internet.

EDIT: Hmm, for some reason the greentexted part of the quote is not showing. Oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a couple similar ones here and there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a really novel and engaging read. I don't think I've found an SCP like this before. Idea was great, execution was good as well

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless I'm wrong about my idea of DNA analysis, how can the blood show he's exactly 18 years old?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I also agree with Famine (way too much arguing and talk about mutilation, the end didnt really have closure for me) and Peppers, why is nothing being done about all the supposedly violent -2 instances?

item.title: Re: SCP-2089
item.content:encoded:
Well there goes the Todd in the Shadows Joke SCP I had planned!
(which I never intended to publish anyway)

Brilliant work of dark satire, this. Vloggers get some serious trolls, and this pretty much hits the nail on the head. Upvote.

One nitpick: "Audio samples taken from videos of SCP-2089 -1 have proven inconclusive in supporting this claim." Should perhaps be a footnote of "claims to be male." The gender of this SCP is largely irrelevant, even in comparison to most SCPs, given that the humanoid portion of it isn't physically contained by the Foundation. It feels odd to have two sentences about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this one. It felt sort of like the online site that makes people commit suicide then adds them to its community. The video comments and such felt very much like stuff people online would actually do, and even though I agree with the stuff posted above by PeppersGhost, at least in regards to making it more obviously anomalous. I also think that the very last bit with the second guy is cool in theory, but I'm not sure how it could be improved. It's definitely janky as it is, though.

I did like it enough to upvote. Even with its flaws, I was immersed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way too much exposition for my liking. Feels like it could have been more effective if it was shorter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really want to like this one. I'm a huge advocate for net-based anomalies, and the idea of a tumblr skip puts a huge grin on my face.

However, I'm down voting this article. It has a lot of great concepts in place, but it also suffers from some major flaws, and I really want to see it reworked to its full potential. As it stands, the article's structure, formatting, and tone feel really sloppy.

  • Why is the SCP the blog? SCP designations are given to anomalies, and there doesn't appear to be anything anomalous at all about the blog itself.
  • As the foundation agent so kindly pointed out, there's no suggestion that the Foundation has any evidence that -1 is anomalous. Lampshade hanging doesn't make up for the sheet nonsensical logic at play here.
  • It's enough of a stretch that the Foundation is simply assuming -1 is anomalous. The idea of them making the unfathable leap of just assuming that -2 are anomalous is insane. All of the described behaviors simply sound like par for the tumblr course.
  • If anything here should be designated an SCP, it's the geographical anomaly. Use that as the main skip and give everything else as background information.
  • The sequence at the end is written in a really weird way. It feels more like a tale than an addendum. Instead of jumping between blocked quotes and narration, why not put the whole thing in the simple, standard video transcript log we use almost everywhere else?

EDIT: June 2014 PeppersGhost is a big grump. His criticism still stands, but this is original and I enjoyed it, so it deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it quite a bit except for the note from Agent Thompson. It's really unclear what the note is saying is happening when they try to go to his house. I'm also unsure why the Foundation assumes that -2 exists. If it's mildly unclear if there's anything anomalous really going on with 2089, it's extremely unclear with the -2s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it as a draft. Like it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. Had some flavor to it, didn't feel boring.
